popularStory.headline: Walter Devereux
popularStory.description: Walter Devereux was an Anglo-Norman nobleman living during the reign of king Henry II of England and Richard I of England. The Devereux, along with the Baskervilles and Pichards, were prominent knight …

zh.Kington, Herefordshire, England: 英國英格蘭賀福爾金頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Eustace Devereux Of Hereford於1173年出生時，他的父親William Devereux45歲，母親Heloise de Lacy41歲。他和Cecilia de Longchamp至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於大約1197年享年25歲。

zh-hans.Kington, Herefordshire, England: 英国英格兰賀福爾金頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eustace Devereux Of Hereford于1173年出生时，他的父亲William Devereux45岁，母亲Heloise de Lacy41岁。他和Cecilia de Longchamp至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于大约1197年享年25岁。

nameMeanings.name: Devereux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Sean.
English and Irish: of Norman origin, a habitational name for someone from Évreux in Eure, France, with the preposition d(e). See also Everest for the name without the preposition.

History: This was the surname of the Earls of Essex from 1572 to 1641. Walter Devereux, 1st Earl of Essex, led an attempted colonization of Ulster, and his son, Robert, 2nd Earl of Essex, briefly held the position of lord lieutenant of Ireland in 1599.

variants.name: Devereaux
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Everest
meanings: English (southeastern): of Norman origin, a variant of Devereux without the fused preposition (Old French de). Compare Evers .

variants.name: Evers
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Devereux without the fused preposition (Old French de).

English: de-aspirated variant of Heaver, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a habitational name from Hever (Kent), named from Old English hēah ‘high’ + (Kentish) Old English ēfre ‘edge or brow of a hill’. Alternatively, the name may derive from some minor place in southeastern England named with Old English ȳfre, (Kentish) Old English ēfre, Middle English evere ‘edge or brow of a hill, tip of a promontory’, with the addition of a prosthetic H-.
North German and Dutch: genitivized patronymic from the personal name Evert , which lost its t in pronunciation and spelling subsequently; compare Everts . Compare also Avers .

variants.name: Deveraux
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Devery
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Kieran, Brendan, Clancy.
Irish (Offaly): of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Devary
meanings: Probably Irish: variant of Devery, a variant of Devereux in counties Leix and Offaly.

variants.name: Debrow
meanings: Altered form of English or Irish   Devereux  .

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Hereford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Hertford (Hertfordshire) (in which the t was lost early, as in the modern pronunciation Harford), Hereford (Herefordshire), or Harford (Devon, Gloucestershire). The Devon and Herefordshire placenames derive from Old English here ‘army’ + ford ‘ford’, while the Gloucestershire and Hertfordshire placenames derive from Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + ford.

variants.name: Herford
meanings: English: variant of   Hereford  .
German: variant of   Herfurth  .
German: habitational name from a place of that name in Westphalia.

variants.name: Heryford
meanings: Altered form of English   Hereford  .

variants.name: Heriford
meanings: Altered form of English   Hereford  .

variants.name: Harford
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): variant of   Hereford  .

variants.name: Havard
meanings: English and Welsh (Brecknockshire and Glamorgan): habitational name from Haverfordwest (Pembrokeshire), from Old English hæfer ‘goat’ + ford ‘ford’.
English and Welsh (Brecknockshire and Glamorgan): in some cases, the name may also be a variant of   Hereford  .
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hahward, composed of the elements hah, which is unexplained, and ward ‘guard’.

variants.name: Hurford
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): variant of   Hereford  .

nameMeanings.name: Eustace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Old French form of the Late Greek names Eustakhios and Eustathios. These were evidently of separate origin, the former derived from eu ‘well, good’ + stakhys ‘grapes’, the latter from eu + stēnai ‘to stand’. However, the tradition is very confused. The name was introduced in this form to Britain by the Normans, among whom it was popular as a result of the fame of St Eustace , a Norman nobleman who was said to have been converted to Christianity by the vision of a crucifix between the antlers of the stag he was hunting.

en.lifeSummary: When Eustace Devereux Of Hereford was born in 1173, his father, William Devereux, was 45 and his mother, Heloise de Lacy, was 41. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Cecilia de Longchamp. He died about 1197, at the age of 25.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eustace Devereux Of Hereford est né en 1173, son père, William Devereux, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Heloise de Lacy, avait 41 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Cecilia de Longchamp. Il est décédé environ 1197, à l’âge de 25 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eustace Devereux Of Hereford 1173 geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Devereux 45 und seine Mutter Heloise de Lacy 41. Er hatte mit Cecilia de Longchamp mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1197 im Alter von 25 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eustace Devereux Of Hereford è nato il nel 1173, il suo padre, William Devereux, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Heloise de Lacy, aveva 41 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Cecilia de Longchamp. Morì circa 1197, all'età di 25 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eustace Devereux Of Herefordが1173年に生まれた時，父William Devereuxは45歳，母Heloise de Lacyは41歳であった。彼はCecilia de Longchampとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は約1197年に25歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eustace Devereux Of Hereford(이)가 1173년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Devereux(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Heloise de Lacy(은)는 41세였다. 그는 Cecilia de Longchamp(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1197년 25세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Kington, Herefordshire, England: Kington, Herefordscīr, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eustace Devereux Of Hereford nasceu em 1173, seu pai, William Devereux, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Heloise de Lacy, tinha 41 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Cecilia de Longchamp. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1197, com 25 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eustace Devereux Of Hereford родился в 1173 г., его отцу по имени William Devereux было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Heloise de Lacy, 41. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Cecilia de Longchamp. Он умер около 1197 г. в возрасте 25 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eustace Devereux Of Hereford nació en 1173, su papá, William Devereux, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Heloise de Lacy, tenía 41 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Cecilia de Longchamp. Murió aproximadamente 1197, a la edad de 25 años.
